title: IU Global Learning Showcase
location: STEPHEN L. & CONNIE J. FERGUSON INTERNATIONAL CENTER - Room Number: 4th Floor
description: May 1, 2026, 9:30 AM - 12:30 PMFerguson International Center, 4th FloorPoster Set-up: 8:45–9:20 AM  Light Breakfast: 9:00–9:30 AM  Keynote Address by Judd Devermont: 9:30–10:45 AM  Poster Session: 11:00 AM – 12:30 PM  Discover Global Learning at IU Bloomington!  Join us for the IU Global Learning Showcase, a celebration of student and faculty innovation in globally engaged education.  This year's keynote address, "Reimagining Strategies Toward Sub-Saharan Africa: Opportunities for U.S. Cities, Communities, and Universities," will be delivered by Judd Devermont, former National Security Council Senior Director for African Affairs and National Intelligence Officer for Africa. He will share lessons learned from drafting the Biden Administration's strategy toward the region and discuss opportunities for Indiana's institutions, leaders, and citizens to model a new, more mutually advantageous partnership.  After the keynote, explore a poster sessionfeaturing presentations from global client projects, Collaborative Online International Learning (COIL), Diplomacy Lab, and more. Topics span disciplines and highlight how IU students and faculty are turning learning into action on some of today's most pressing global challenges.All are welcome—come be inspired and join us in shaping a more connected world!  Present Your Work at the Poster SessionAre you a student or instructor engaged in global learning on an IU campus? We invite you to share your work during the poster session! This is a great opportunity to showcase experiential learning projects, connect with others, and highlight how global learning is happening right here in Indiana. Poster printing costs will be covered.   Please fill out this brief interest form (https://iuglobal-fireform.eas.iu.edu/online/form/index/cgcgl002) if you would like to participate in the poster session.  Questions?  Email global@iu.edu (mailto:global@iu.edu) with Global Learning Showcase in the subject line.Supported by: IU Global, the Center for the Study of Global Change
source: IU Hamilton Lugar School, IU Kelley School of Business

title: Arbor Day 2021 Native Tree Seedling Giveaway sponsored by IU Health
Awakening Earth: A Spring Arts Gathering at Sage Valley
location: 9975 N State Rd 67, Worthington, IN, United States, Indiana 47471
description:
As the land begins to wake up and the wildflowers bloom, it’s time for us to come together once again. We are thrilled to invite you to our 9th annual Spring Gathering, a four-day immersive celebration of music, art, and the spirit of community.

This isn't just a festival; it’s a transformative experience where we bridge the gap between artist and observer, neighbor and friend. Whether you are here for the workshops, the music, or the simple peace of camping under the stars, there is a place for you here.

What to expect:

We begin Friday night with an opening fire circle and informal jam sessions. Feel free to bring your instruments and play around the fireside, or simply just vibe.

Create & Learn: Dive into workshops led by incredible talent, including painting, flow arts, drumming, and storytelling.

Enjoy diverse live performances from local and traveling musicians throughout the weekend.

Family Magic: From the Mystical March costume parade and the Kid's Glow Party to the Sunday Variety Show, our Kid Village is designed to spark wonder in our youngest community members.

A Gathering for Everyone
In keeping with our values of inclusion and accessibility, this event is donation-based with free camping. We believe that financial barriers should never stand in the way of cultural connection.

It is our team of dedicated volunteers and donors who keep this space running, so there are many ways to get involved including musical performances, workshop leadership, vending, and more in exchange for admission.

🎶 Stage Request Form:
https://forms.gle/p6BN9YxxisC1yf7L6

🛍️ Vendors:
https://forms.gle/sw819oxZACBv4yCu5

🤸🏽‍♂️Workshop Leaders:
https://forms.gle/TDpvNbFitqtkmqXN6

🤹‍♀️ Children's Playshop Leaders:
https://forms.gle/YztG9Q4vHxqWqMHB8

👐 Volunteer Request Form:
https://forms.gle/EV4nnrPPnN3ZCGCcA

ALL individuals 16+ must fill out a Registration Form in order to enter the event. There will be a device at the gate to fill it out upon arrival, a QR code to scan to fill it out on your own device, or you can fill it out now to save some time once you arrive!

Everyone will need to fill this out regardless if you are a volunteer, a vendor, resident, stage artist, have attended events at Sage Valley before, or any other circumstance. This is NOT the same as the other links above! We must have one completed for ALL event attendees in addition to your ticket, volunteer, stage, and/or vendor form.

✍️ Registration Form: https://forms.gle/T4xqrEE2RXc9tVLC6

This gathering is hosted by Sage Valley, a 501(c)3 non-profit organization. All proceeds go towards creating the event and growing the projects and programs at Sage Valley! Donations of time, resources, or monetary energy beyond the suggested donation are encouraged.

Suggested donation to camp at this event is on a sliding scale of $30-$100 per adult or family, in addition to one or more items to donate to our vegan kitchen, which provides free meals throughout the event.

Children 15 years old and under require NO admission donation. Minors must be accompanied by a legal guardian.

We do accept alternatives to monetary donations, here's our list of acceptable donation items: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lXlLmt3BiOfK6FckOlQ8j5zSouwGEaDCURkqN_Mzivs/edit?usp=sharing

FAQ
----------
➸11:11 MEETINGS
On Friday, Saturday, and Sunday there will be a community-wide meeting at 11:11 am! This is the time to receive all the information you need to make your experience as wonderful as possible. Please aim to attend daily to stay updated on the day's flow, ask questions, offer your input, and get to know our team and community members!

➸GATE HOURS
The gate officially opens at 10AM on Friday, May 1st. ((Early entry will ONLY be allowed for registered and pre-approved volunteers.)) The gate will be closed at 9PM each night to ensure safety. Entry after 9PM will not be permitted.

DRIVE SLOWLY when approaching the gate, the road is curved at the gate entry and on a hill!

➸WHY MONETARY DONATION? WHY FOOD DONATION?

Admission and kitchen donations ensure we are all giving what we can. As a nonprofit, No profits are taken from donations. Donations go into creating the most magical gatherings we can now and in the future. Hosting a gathering of this magnitude takes resources and funding. Our goal is to provide as much as we can in a sustainable way. Donations can be made online and at the gate.

Everyone entering the event grounds will need to give a donation of money, time, or accepted food to the community. If you find the suggested minimum donation unaffordable, there are volunteer opportunities and other creative alternative donations for admission.

IMPORTANT NOTE: ALL ONLINE DONATIONS ARE FINAL, NON-REFUNDABLE, & NON-RETURNABLE.

By donating you understand & agree to all the information provided on the donation/ticket website. Tickets are transferable & you are encouraged to gift your ticket if circumstances shift and you're unable to attend.

➸CAMPING, RVs & BUSES
This are NO hookups available at Sage Valley.

There are designated spaces spaces for RVs, busses, and Vans. They'll be pointed out to you join arrival!

➸CAMPING
Sage Valley is off-grid and camping is PRIMITIVE. Please plan accordingly. There are several options for your camping experience including areas for car camping, tent camping, family camping, & forest camping. (Forest camping requires all gear to be hiked in as it is not accessible by vehicles. If you need help with transporting gear, we have a UTV and may be able to help! Just let those at gate check-in know.)

➸FOOD AND WATER
Sage Valley's vegan kitchen will provide plant based meals throughout the weekend! You're welcome to bring in additional food for nourishment between provided meals. Though wildlife is minimal during gathering season, please prepare as if you were camping anywhere outdoors. If you need advice on how to store your food, let someone know!

You may use a small camp stove at your campsite. You may NOT build a fire at your campsite unless there is a fire pit already present when you arrive. You may NOT cook at the sacred fire. If you need a fire for cooking please let someone know and we will show you where to go!

Sage Valley's water is pumped from the well underneath the land and filtered for our drinking. You're encouraged to bring your own water in addition!

➸DRIVING/PARKING DURING EVENT
View our Driving/Parking Policy here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/15QonFI5hLlNJzZVp933DeYyaUrGZs8gOzNg14hh2ePI/edit?usp=sharing.

➸VENDORS
Come one and come all vendors! We encourage vending of your DIY and homemade crafts, art, services, foods, and more! If you are interested in vending, please register here so that we may plan the space accordingly and have a spot reserved for you: https://forms.gle/6kiQoDN4h3eM6BLH9

➸SECURITY & SAFETY
This is primarily a self-regulated event where attendees are expected to speak out and stand up for what is right. Appointed Peace Keepers will be available throughout the event to promote safety. We take care of each other and strive to create a safe space for all! Please be sure to respect and listen to one another.

➸HANDICAP ACCESSIBILITY
We do everything we can to make this event as accessible as possible to all. Parking is within walking distance from the main event area, however, the terrain is naturally very uneven. If you have any special needs to be considered, please reach out to the event organizers so that we may try our best to accommodate you.

➸LEAVE-NO-TRACE
This is a LEAVE-NO-TRACE event! It's simple: leave the place you camp the same or better than you found it. Pack it in and pack it out. This property is an actual ecovillage where people live. Please treat it the way you would want others to treat your own home. If you see others not honoring the LEAVE-NO-TRACE motto, take some time to explain to them why it's important & nicely ask them to pick up after themselves. All trash brought onto the land must be carried off of the land. Trash removal costs money & uses resources, energy, and time. Attendees found disrupting the natural beauty of the land will be prompt

title: Global Competencies Skills Development Series: Early Lunch: Strategic Thinking in U.S.-Africa Policy and Intelligence
location: GLOBAL & INTERNATIONAL STUDIES BUILDING - Room Number: 1118
description: Event Description:  How do policymakers and intelligence officers navigate complex decisions in Africa and beyond?  Join Judd Devermont, former National Intelligence Officer for Africa and former Senior Director for African Affairs at the White House, for a live, scenario-driven session on strategy, risk, and global policymaking.  Devermont spent over a decade in the intelligence community, advised two administrations, authored the U.S. Strategy toward Sub-Saharan Africa, and directed major initiatives shaping billions in infrastructure, digital connectivity, and energy investments.  This is not a lecture.  You will work through a real-world scenario: African states balancing relationships with the U.S. and China while managing domestic political and development pressures.  You'll assess risk, identify second- and third-order effects, evaluate trade-offs, and recommend a course of action.What you'll gain:      Insight into how intelligence and policy intersect          Frameworks to anticipate unintended consequences          How analysis translates into real-world decisions          A window into careers in intelligence, government, and think tanks      Lunch will be provided. Come ready to debate, analyze, and defend your conclusions in an interactive, off-the-record setting.
source: IU Hamilton Lugar School, IU Kelley School of Business

title: Tour of Thomas Hart Benton's Indiana Murals
location: AUDITORIUM - Room Number: Hall of Murals
description: This tour visitsThe Hall of Murals at IU Auditorium  IU Cinema  Woodburn Hall  About the Indiana Murals:  Thomas Hart Benton was commissioned to paint murals depicting Indiana history for the Indiana state pavilion at the 1933 World's Fair in Chicago. The dramatic 250-foot long mural, which originally wrapped around the entire exhibition hall at the World's Fair, depicts the social, economic, and cultural history of Indiana from mound building to the 1930s. After several years of storage following the fair, the panels were given to Indiana University in 1938, where they were installed in three locations on the Bloomington campus. About Your Guide:  Jim Davis is a Senior Lecturer in the Kelley School of Business, in the department of Management and Entrepreneurship. He has been serving as a docent and museum guide at the IU Eskenazi Museum of Art for the past ten years.     Know Before You GoThis tour visits three locations on campus. Please let us know if an elevator and wheelchair accessible route will be needed.   Plan to arrive at least 5 minutes beforehand to check in.For a discounted parking rate for the Indiana Memorial Union parking lot, visit the Eskenazi Museum of Art before or after this tour and ask Guest Services, at the Information Desk on the first floor, to stamp your parking voucher.

title: Writing in the Galleries
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Check in on the first floor
description: In this workshop, we begin by looking closely at artwork—asking what we see, think, feel, and wonder. Together, we'll explore the artist's possible message and its social meaning, then turn those reflections into our own creative responses.  Whether you're a writer or simply curious to try something new, you'll find inspiration here and a welcoming community to share the journey.  What to Bringyour favorite pencil (no pens).  a notebook or paper. Clipboards are available to borrow.  a phone or camera to take a picture of the art for later reference.  Know Before You GoArrive early, check in, and get ready for an on-time start.Readwhere to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines.About Your Facilitator  Dedaimia Whitney earned an MFA in fiction from Indiana University under the direction of Scott Russell Sanders. She taught creative writing at Franklin College for many years. She has served the museum as a docent since 2019. Her favorite works of art are those that are both visually stimulating and thought-provoking.
source: Eskenazi Museum of Art, IU Arts & Humanities Institute

title: Beginner/Intermediate Drawing Workshop
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Check in with Guest Services on the first floor
description: Reserve your spot. If this page says, "Registration has closed" that means the waitlist is full.SuppliesBring your favorite drawing pencil (e.g. 4B), a sketchbook or drawing paper, and a phone or device to photograph artwork (without flash) as we sketch.  Know Before You GoArrive early to find parking, check in, and get your drawing supplies ready.  At 1:55 p.m. any unclaimed reservations will be released to guests who are standing by waiting for a spot.  Where to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines (https://artmuseum.indiana.edu/visit/your-visit.html).  This workshop is recommended for ages 12 and up. Anyone under 18, who is not an IU student, must be accompanied by an adult.  When You ArriveCheck in with Guest Services at the information desk on the first floor, even if you come often and even if you are late. We appreciate having an accurate attendance count.  Store all backpacks and large bags before entering the galleries. A small handbag may be carried at your side.  Get your supplies ready. Attach an 18x24" piece of newsprint to a drawing board.  Cancellations  This workshop often has a waitlist. Please use the cancellation link included with your confirmation email if your plans change.About the Instructor  John Cheek has taught credit and non-credit classes for eight years in portrait painting, landscape painting, palette knife painting, life drawing, beginning drawing, and advanced drawing, as well as in design. He volunteers his time and expertise to facilitate drawing workshops at the Eskenazi Museum of Art.
source: Eskenazi Museum of Art, IU Arts & Humanities Institute

title: Family Art Lab
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Roehm Family Art-making Studio
description: Please note that this month's Family Art Lab does not follow the second Sunday schedule.  Designed for K-12 students and their families, this event offers a fun and flexible way to spark creativity and connect with each other. All ages and experience levels are welcome!  Come and go at your own pace. We recommend planning for at least an hour to explore the gallery and make art.  Start in the Art Studio inside the Kimberly and John Simpson Center for Education on the museum's 2nd floor to pick up your Gallery Challenge. Then head to the galleries to find the artwork we'll use as inspiration for our project! When you're ready, head back to the studio for our hands-on art-making activity that everyone will love.  This program takes place every Second Sunday of the month, excluding holidays and museum closures. Each month features new artworks and materials, so there's always something fresh to explore and create!  All children must be chaperoned.Know Before You Go:Read about where to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines.Strollers are welcome in the galleries. When carrying small children, please keep them in front of you.  To save time at the lockers, avoid bringing backpacks or large bags.  When You Arrive:If you parked in the IMU parking lot, Guest Services can validate your parking voucher for a discount.  Store any large bags in the lockers behind the Guest Services desk.  Head upstairs to the 2nd Floor and into the Simpson Center for Education to start.  When in the galleries, do not touch the artwork. Be mindful to keep body parts and anything you're carrying at least 18" away from the art.  Photography is welcome! Just no flash or selfie sticks.

title: Finn: A New Musical – At the PB&J Cafe
Exploring Music in Art
location: Eskenazi Museum of Art - Room Number: Check in at the information desk on the first floor
description: Depending on what's on view, this tour may include a Māori flute, Shiva (Lord of the Dance), drums from the Austral Islands and New Ireland, a Mayan bell, a Kango whistle, a Lumbo bow lute, a Mangbeta friction drum, an Egyptian castanet, sculptures of seated female Chinese musicians playing a pipa and a piaxiao, a biwat flute stop, and a gope board to play music from New Guinea sent to space on Voyager's Golden Record.    About your guide:  Kathie Durkel has volunteered as a Guide at the Eskenazi Museum of Art for more than ten years. She is the 2023 recipient of the Meier Award in recognition for her exemplary service, enthusiasm,and commitment to learning. She enjoys creating thematic tours, like this one.      Know Before You GoSpace is available for the first 12 people to check in with Guest Services on the day. title: Lincoln's Declaration: How He Interpreted--and Defended--Our Founding Document
location: Unitarian Universalist Church, 2120 N Fee Ln, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description: In honor of its 250th Anniversary, John Schilb, Culbertson Chair and Professor Emeritus of English, will analyze Abraham Lincoln’s rhetoric regarding the Declaration of Independence. Many of Lincoln's opponents (e.g., Stephen Douglas and Jefferson Davis) either ridiculed the Declaration's claim that "all men are created equal" or believed that it applied just to whites. Lincoln maintained that "all" really did mean everyone. Only gradually, however, was he willing to consider Black people his social peers. Statements that he made during his debates with Douglas reflect this complex attitude. And even the Gettysburg Address does not assume that the Declaration's message has a future. Indeed, that speech called the Declaration's claim about equality a "proposition" to be tested. That is, Lincoln saw American democracy as something that might not endure. Its fate still seems in question, so his take on the Declaration remains timely.

title: AI Applications in Marketing
description: 2026 Dates: May 5-May 28Tuesdays and Thursdays from 6 to 7:30 p.m. ETDelivery: Online, instructor-ledPrice: $1,995  Equip yourself with a GenAI toolset designed for impactful AI adoption. Anchored in the marketing process, this course empowers you with skills to leverage AI effectively, driving growth, reducing cycle times, and cutting costs. Dive deep into the market landscape, employ advanced analytics, create compelling content, and utilize GenAI tools for automation and monitoring. You'll culminate your learning by creating a personalized marketing AI assistant, all while mastering change management for competitive advantages.  Who is this for? Marketing practitioners and leaders who are hungry to get up to speed on AI for themselves, their teams, and organizations, and don't want to be left behind. Anchored in the marketing planning process, students will learn how AI can help answer marketing questions through hands-on learning and case studies. AI enables students to build apps and actions that were previously the domain of coders. No prior AI experience or technical experience is needed to take full advantage of the course.

description:
Every Wednesday at 8PM is Comedy Attic Open Mic Night. Bloomington has one of the best local comedy scenes in the country, and it all starts with our open mic night!

Each comedian is asked to do no more than five minutes of completely original material. While we do not care about foul language in your set, we do not allow racist, homophobic, transphobic or rape/female bashing jokes. If you have to ask if it's racist, homophobic, transphobic or a rape joke, it probably is.

Comedians that wish to participate are asked to e-mail comedyattic@gmail.com mailto:comedyattic@gmail.com . Newcomers and any participants who are not paid professionals will be required to bring audience members to see them. This is non-negotiable. So if you plan on signing up, be sure to invite as many friends as you can. Good luck!

title: Your Name Means Dream by Jose Rivera
location: Waldron Rose Firebay,122 South Walnut Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
AI is hauntingly current and a topic that urges us to examine its impact.
Will AI robots soon replace human caregivers for the elderly? With emotionally-charged narrative and poetic dialogue, José Rivera examines how we live and form our sense of self in a world that’s increasingly saturated with technology. It challenges all people, all identities and all faiths. How do we apply morals, values and ethics when we introduce machine over human?

Theatre legend Diane Kondrat, who moved west after performing theatre for 27 years in Bloomington, is returning to star in this production. The role of AI caregiver Stacy will be played by Valerie C. Kilmer who is making her debut with JTB.

With an extraordinary team of artists and a fascinating topic, this production promises to be a sell-out!

title: Wilderness First Aid Courses
description:
Our Wilderness First Aid (WFA) courses will give you the skills needed to respond to medical emergencies when help is far away.
This is a 2-day/16-hour course that features extensive hands-on learning opportunities. Students will walk away with a 2-year WFA certification from Stonehearth Open Learning Opportunities (SOLO), which is one of the oldest wilderness medicine organizations in the world.
Students may also add on CPR modules and/or re-certify for their SOLO Wilderness First Responder (WFR) certification.
Courses start at $275. Cost includes course materials and lunch on Saturday and Sunday. Overnight lodging from Friday–Sunday may be available for participants on a first-come, first-serve basis.
Register for the course here.

Hosted by: Bradford Woods

Additional Information can be found at: https://beinvolved.indiana.edu/event/12122726

title: 2nd Weekend Work Day Trail Building
description:
Knobstone Hiking Trail Association of Indiana Meetup
Would you to spend a day in some beautiful woods with a delightful group of people building a connecting section of Indiana’s premier long distance hiking trail? Come join us as we continue work on the Tecumseh Trail extension portion of the KHT in MMSF. We will be building the new trail including clipping green briar.
Please bring a lunch and water. We will have large hand tools, but please bring any small tools you have such as clippers or a pruning saw. And a backpack is handy to carry everything in. Instructions will be provided for those who need them. We finish about 3:30.
Meet at the Hatfield Ridge Road gate. The gate is locked but we have a key. Go to the big sign on Rosenbaum/Catholic Cemetry Rd.
Join us on our website at http://www.knobstonehikingtrail.org/, on Facebook at [www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484](http://www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484), and Substack [https://khta.substack.com/](https://khta.substack.com/)
source: Meetup Knobstone Hiking Trail Association Of Indiana Meetup

title: Some Economics of the Social Safety Net
description:
Over 40 million people in America receive SNAP (food stamp) benefits, over 70 million are enrolled in Medicaid, and about 9 million receive Section 8 Housing Vouchers. Gerhard Glomm, Professor of Economics, will discuss some of the design features of these programs, with an emphasis on relatively recent changes such as work requirements. He will then share data on the costs and benefits of these programs. This event will be held at the Brown County Public Library, 205 Locust Lane, Nashville, IN.

https://www.facebook.com/events/25935694006040900/

title: Finn: A New Musical- At the PB&J Cafe
location: Waldron Auditorium,122 South Walnut Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
Constellation Stage & Screen presents “Finn: A New Musical- At the PB&J Cafe,” on stage May 1-17 at the Waldron Auditorium.

From the creator of Doc McStuffins and Vampirina, Finn is a heartwarming musical adventure about a young shark on the brink of growing up. As he prepares to join the fearsome Shark Guard, Finn can’t shake the feeling that he’s different from the other sharks. Deep inside, he discovers a colorful fish inside him—a part of himself that longs to sparkle, sing, and dance. As Finn embarks on a journey of self-discovery, friendship, and adventure, he finds the courage to be himself! Fun for all ages!

PB&J Cafe: A Dinner Theater for Kids! At the PB&J Cafe, young theatergoers will enjoy a meal during the performance and leave with autographs from their favorite characters.

See Constellation’s website for more information, including a detailed performance schedule. Learn more and find tickets at seeconstellation.org/kids/finn/ https://seeconstellation.org/kids/finn/ https://seeconstellation.org/kids/finn/

title: Homeschool Day: America 250
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART
description: Travel back in time with us to America in 1776 to meet George Washington and see the Declaration of Independence up close. Together, we'll explore the art and stories that shaped our nation's founding. Afterwards, we'll create our own colonial-inspired artwork in the studio, reflecting on the theme of "What America Means to Me."Know Before You Go:  Meet us on the 1st floor of the museum at 10 a.m. Together, we'll drop off personal items in the Simpson Center for Education before exploring the galleries. After seeing artworks that will inspire our project, we'll head back to the Roehm Family Art-Making Studio to create our own masterpieces! Don't forget to dress for a mess!  Strollers are welcome. Chaperones required. We ask that chaperones be 18 or older, with one adult for every 6 children.Parking Information:  The museum is in the pedestrian district of campus with several parking options nearby. The Indiana Memorial Union lot (900 E. 7th Street) is just a 5-minute walk diagonal to the museum, and visitors can receive 40% off parking validation at the museum's guest services desk. The East Garage (105 N. Eagleson Ave.) and Fee Lane Garage (709 N. Fee Lane) are also convenient, about an 8–10 minute walk. Learn more about parking on the Bloomington campus here (https://parking.indiana.edu/facilities/index.html).

title: Spring Moth Night
location: Griffy Lake Nature Preserve, 3400 N Headley Rd, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
Registration fee $6/in-city, $7/non-city. Register online by May 13 at https://webtrac.bloomington.in.gov/webtrac/web/search.html?Primarycode=140016. For ages 10 yrs. and up.
Late spring-early summer is an excellent time to observe moths. These insects overwinter in a pupal case under the leaf litter, and arise in the springtime as beautiful moths. Spring is the season when you are most likely to find cecropia and luna moths. Lets see what we can find! Participants should plan to bring portable seating and a flashlight and be able to hike on a moderate trail.

title: Pre-College Strings Violins – Spring Gala Concert
A Rock n’ Roll Tribute from Elvis to The Beatles featuring The Neverly Brothers
location: Brown County Playhouse, 70 S. Van Buren St, Nashville, IN, 47448, United States
description:
“A Rock ‘n’ Roll Tribute – From Elvis to the Beatles featuring The Neverly Brothers”
The Neverly Brothers concerts take you on a guided musical VIRTUAL TOUR of the birth, near death and resurrection of rock n’ roll – from 1955 to 1965. You’ll learn rock history and be thoroughly entertained with their chronological song sequence, stories behind the music and vintage wardrobe changes. Their energetic stage show consists of show-stopping renditions of early hits by Jerry Lee Lewis, Chuck Berry, Buddy Holly, The Beatles, Rolling Stones, and more!
The first half of their concert pays tribute to the music created by the 1950s American Rock n’ Roll pioneers – which by 1960 had become a distant memory but became the inspiration for the next wave of young rock bands from Britain. The second half then pays tribute to the 1964 British Invasion bands that re-recorded the forgotten 1950s American rock music – along with lesser known early 1960s rhythm & blues songs, recorded their own original songs and pulled Rock n’ Roll from the edge of extinction by re-inventing and re-energizing it for a new generation.
Their repertoire includes spirited performances of classics like Great balls of fire, Johnny B. Goode, Wake up little Susie, Summertime blues, That’ll be the day, Twist & shout, Roll over Beethoven, Satisfaction, You really got me, Love potion #9 and many more!
It‘s not “moldy oldies”- it’s “old-school cool!” So hop aboard our virtual tour bus and fasten your seatbelts for an exciting MAGICAL HISTORY TOUR!

BUY TICKETS!

title: Your Name Means Dream by Jose Rivera
location: Waldron Rose Firebay,122 South Walnut Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
AI is hauntingly current and a topic that urges us to examine its impact.
Will AI robots soon replace human caregivers for the elderly? With emotionally-charged narrative and poetic dialogue, José Rivera examines how we live and form our sense of self in a world that’s increasingly saturated with technology. It challenges all people, all identities and all faiths. How do we apply morals, values and ethics when we introduce machine over human?

Theatre legend Diane Kondrat, who moved west after performing theatre for 27 years in Bloomington, is returning to star in this production. The role of AI caregiver Stacy will be played by Valerie C. Kilmer who is making her debut with JTB.

With an extraordinary team of artists and a fascinating topic, this production promises to be a sell-out!

title: Finn: A New Musical- At the PB&J Cafe
location: Waldron Auditorium,122 South Walnut Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
Constellation Stage & Screen presents “Finn: A New Musical- At the PB&J Cafe,” on stage May 1-17 at the Waldron Auditorium.

From the creator of Doc McStuffins and Vampirina, Finn is a heartwarming musical adventure about a young shark on the brink of growing up. As he prepares to join the fearsome Shark Guard, Finn can’t shake the feeling that he’s different from the other sharks. Deep inside, he discovers a colorful fish inside him—a part of himself that longs to sparkle, sing, and dance. As Finn embarks on a journey of self-discovery, friendship, and adventure, he finds the courage to be himself! Fun for all ages!

PB&J Cafe: A Dinner Theater for Kids! At the PB&J Cafe, young theatergoers will enjoy a meal during the performance and leave with autographs from their favorite characters.

title: Whose Live Anyway?
location: Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
WHOSE LIVE ANYWAY?

The current cast members of the Emmy-nominated TV show Whose Line Is It Anyway? are proud to present their new improv tour: WHOSE LIVE ANYWAY?

WHOSE LIVE ANYWAY? is 90 minutes of hilarious improvised comedy and song all based on audience suggestions. Cast members Ryan Stiles, Greg Proops, Jeff B. Davis, and Joel Murray will leave you gasping with the very witty scenes they invent before your eyes. Audience participation is key to the show so bring your suggestions and you might be asked to join the cast onstage! WHOSE LIVE ANYWAY? showcases some of the improv games made famous on the long-running TV show as well as some exciting new ones, featuring musical direction by Laura Hall.

All ages are welcomed, but please note that some “PG-13” language will be used during the performance.

description:

Each comedian is asked to do no more than five minutes of completely original material. So if you plan on signing up, be sure to invite as many friends as you can. Good luck!

location: Wells Screening Room (ground floor, within Media Services)

title: You (probably) Haven't Seen This Before
description: Dive deep into the archival collections of IU Libraries Moving Image Archive through a new monthly screening series of short films! Each month, rare and unique archival films will be presented focusing on underrepresented narratives and underrepresented motion-picture practices that push on and expand the spectrum of what is considered "cinema."  In an effort to keep audiences open-minded and with limited pre-conceptions of these rare works of celluloid, descriptions of films to be shown will not be shared prior to screenings. Join us based on announced themes and the expectation that the works you see will fall outside of Hollywood tropes, outside of mainstream commercial expectations, and outside of conventional filmmaking practices. Trust us, you (probably) haven't seen these films before!  This program is curated by Joan Hawkins and the Writers Guild at Bloomington and hosted by the IU Libraries Moving Image Archive Screening Room.  Please remember to register below.

title: Declaration of Independence Curator Talks
description: The Declaration of Independence: The Motives, The Moment is an exhibition exploring the journey taken by the nation's founders. How colonial leaders go from loyal subjects of the British empire to risking everything by becoming revolutionaries? Take an insightful tour of the exhibition from the curator, with up close looks at Revolutionary War era correspondence, books and manuscripts that influenced the revolutionaries, and media that spread the word about the declaration.  Light refreshments will be offered. Come learn something new about the nation's founding and have a snack in Indiana University's rare book library!  Tours are offered at noon, 1 p.m., and 2 p.m. RSVP requested for planning of refreshments and staffing.

title: Belt Out Loud! - Featuring Cody Ikerd and the Sidewinders
location: Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
Stone Belt is proud to announce The Sixth Annual Belt Out Loud! concert, at 7pm on May 30th. This year’s headlining performers are country music stars Cody Ikerd and the Sidewinders, whose heartfelt songs and captivating stage presence promise an unforgettable evening.

Attendees can enjoy a night of incredible music while supporting a great cause. Belt Out Loud serves as a model for future events aiming to promote accessibility and inclusion for individuals of all abilities, ensuring an enjoyable experience for everyone. By supporting Belt Out Loud, attendees are advocating for a more inclusive society and promoting awareness about the importance of accessibility. Together, we’ll enjoy a night of music and pave the way for a future where every event, big or small, embraces inclusivity as it’s core value.

The proceeds from the event will help Stone Belt provide vital services to individuals with developmental disabilities in our community, including employment training, day and residential services, and advocacy support. So come out and enjoy a fantastic concert while making a positive impact!

This event is proudly sponsored by IU Credit Union, Old National Bank, Office Easel, Tri-County Hydraullics, Resource Services, and Visit Bloomington.

The proceeds from your ticket will help Stone Belt provide vital services to individuals with developmental disabilities in our community, including employment training, day and residential services, and advocacy support. So come out and enjoy a fantastic concert while making a positive impact!

title: Another Revolution
location: Constellation Playhouse,107 West 9th Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
Constellation Stage & Screen presents “Another Revolution,” on stage June 4-21 at the Constellation Playhouse.

Some revolutions happen in the streets. Others in the lab.

Kat and Henry, two graduate students from opposing scientific disciplines, are forced to share a lab at Columbia University in 1968. Amidst interpersonal differences, a campus devolving into political chaos, and the uncertainty and turmoil of the outside world, they each discover what it’s like to be thrown into someone else’s orbit. By Jacqueline Bircher, playwright of Constellation’s hit production of Webster’s B!+@#. Recommended for ages 13+.

See Constellation’s website for more information, including a detailed performance schedule. Learn more and find tickets at seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/ https://seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/ https://seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/

title: Lemonade Day 2026
description:
Lemonade Day is a free, fun, experiential learning program that teaches youth how to start, own and operate their own business – a lemonade stand.
The foremost objective of Lemonade Day is to empower youth to take ownership of their lives and become productive members of society – the business leaders, social advocates, volunteers, and forward thinking citizens of tomorrow.
Each child that registers receives access to Lemonopolis, our FREE and FUN online curriculum that teaches them Lemonade Day lessons like creating budgets, setting profit-making goals, serving customers, repaying investors, and giving back to the community. Along the way, they acquire skills in goal-setting, problem solving, and gain self-esteem critical for future success. They also receive additional digital content from local sponsors and experts on topics like Building a Lemonade Stand, Recipe Exploration, Marketing, and more!

title: Writing in the Galleries
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Check in on the first floor
description: In this workshop, we begin by looking closely at artwork—asking what we see, think, feel, and wonder. Together, we'll explore what we think the artist is trying to say and what the social significance of their message might be. Then we'll use our imaginations to respond to any aspect of our experience. Poets, story-tellers, and essayists are all welcome in this group!  What to Bringyour favorite pencil (no pens).  a notebook or paper. Clipboards are available to borrow.  a phone or camera to take a picture of the art for later reference.  Know Before You GoArrive early, check in, and get ready for an on-time start.Readwhere to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines.About Your Facilitator  Dedaimia Whitney earned an MFA in fiction from Indiana University under the direction of Scott Russell Sanders. She taught creative writing at Franklin College for many years. She has served the museum as a docent since 2019. Her favorite works of art are those that are both visually stimulating and thought-provoking.
source: Eskenazi Museum of Art, IU Arts & Humanities Institute

title: Another Revolution
location: Constellation Playhouse,107 West 9th Street, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
Constellation Stage & Screen presents “Another Revolution,” on stage June 4-21 at the Constellation Playhouse.

Some revolutions happen in the streets. Others in the lab.

Kat and Henry, two graduate students from opposing scientific disciplines, are forced to share a lab at Columbia University in 1968. Amidst interpersonal differences, a campus devolving into political chaos, and the uncertainty and turmoil of the outside world, they each discover what it’s like to be thrown into someone else’s orbit. By Jacqueline Bircher, playwright of Constellation’s hit production of Webster’s B!+@#. Recommended for ages 13+.

See Constellation’s website for more information, including a detailed performance schedule. Learn more and find tickets at seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/ https://seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/ https://seeconstellation.org/mainstage/another-revolution/

title: Beginner/Intermediate Drawing Workshop
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Check in with Guest Services on the first floor
description: Reserve your spot. If this page says, "Registration has closed" that means the waitlist is full.SuppliesBring your favorite drawing pencil (e.g. 4B), a sketchbook or drawing paper, and a phone or device to photograph artwork (without flash) as we sketch.  Know Before You GoArrive early to find parking, check in, and get your drawing supplies ready.  At 1:55 p.m. any unclaimed reservations will be released to guests who are standing by waiting for a spot.  Where to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines (https://artmuseum.indiana.edu/visit/your-visit.html).  This workshop is recommended for ages 12 and up. Anyone under 18, who is not an IU student, must be accompanied by an adult.  When You ArriveCheck in with Guest Services at the information desk on the first floor, even if you come often and even if you are late. We appreciate having an accurate attendance count.  Store all backpacks and large bags before entering the galleries. A small handbag may be carried at your side.  Get your supplies ready. Attach an 18x24" piece of newsprint to a drawing board.  Cancellations  This workshop often has a waitlist. Please use the cancellation link included with your confirmation email if your plans change.About the Instructor  John Cheek has taught credit and non-credit classes for eight years in portrait painting, landscape painting, palette knife painting, life drawing, beginning drawing, and advanced drawing, as well as in design. He volunteers his time and expertise to facilitate drawing workshops at the Eskenazi Museum of Art.
source: Eskenazi Museum of Art, IU Arts & Humanities Institute

location: 6311 North Matthews Drive, Ellettsville, IN 47429, USA
description:
Sharing the art of carving the local limestone, now in it's 30th year. All levels are welcome, from professional to beginner. Carve just one day or up to 2 weeks, stone and instruction included. Held on the grounds of the Bybee Stone Co, Ellettsville.

For more information and to register please visit http://www.limestonesymposium.org

title: Declaration of Independence Curator Talks
description: The Declaration of Independence: The Motives, The Moment is an exhibition exploring the journey taken by the nation's founders. How colonial leaders go from loyal subjects of the British empire to risking everything by becoming revolutionaries? Take an insightful tour of the exhibition from the curator, with up close looks at Revolutionary War era correspondence, books and manuscripts that influenced the revolutionaries, and media that spread the word about the declaration.  Light refreshments will be offered. Come learn something new about the nation's founding and have a snack in Indiana University's rare book library!  Tours are offered at noon, 1 p.m., and 2 p.m. RSVP requested for planning of refreshments and staffing.

title: 2nd Weekend Work Day Trail Building
description:
Knobstone Hiking Trail Association of Indiana Meetup
Would you to spend a day in some beautiful woods with a delightful group of people building a connecting section of Indiana’s premier long distance hiking trail? Come join us as we continue work on the Tecumseh Trail extension portion of the KHT in MMSF. We will be building the new trail including clipping green briar.
Please bring a lunch and water. We will have large hand tools, but please bring any small tools you have such as clippers or a pruning saw. And a backpack is handy to carry everything in. Instructions will be provided for those who need them. We finish about 3:30.
Meet at the Hatfield Ridge Road gate. The gate is locked but we have a key. Go to the big sign on Rosenbaum/Catholic Cemetry Rd.
Join us on our website at http://www.knobstonehikingtrail.org/, on Facebook at [www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484](http://www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484), and Substack [https://khta.substack.com/](https://khta.substack.com/)
source: Meetup Knobstone Hiking Trail Association Of Indiana Meetup

title: Relaxing with Landscapes
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: check in with Guest Services at the information desk on the first floor
description: Cool down your body while heating up your mind as we discuss what we see as a group, helping each other discover details we might otherwise miss in a quick visit through the galleries.    About Your Guide  Dr. TammyJo Eckhart is an ancient historian who has enjoyed being part of the Eskenazi Guide Program since 2009. She was the 2025 recipient of the Patricia and Joel Meier Guide Award from that program. She looks forward to sitting with you and really spending time with these wonderful landscapes.    Know Before You Go  Space is available for the first 12 people to check in with Guest Services on the day. Drop by the information desk on the first floor of the museum when you arrive.A small handbag may be carried at your side. Backpacks and large bags will need to be stored before touring. Learn more aboutmuseum guidelines.If you park in the IMU parking lot, Guest Services can validate your parking voucher for a discount.Read aboutwhere to find us and where to park,

title: Zach Rushing “Redneck Logic” Comedy Tour
location: Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
I’m just a Mississippi boy with a loud mouth and a big heart. I grew up believing in hard work, good friends, and telling it like it is, and I never really grew out of that. Most people know me from my comedy, where I’ve built a career on being unapologetically myself. Whether I’m on stage for the Not-So-Rich-And-Famous Tour, raising hell on social media, or telling stories that hit a little too close to home, I try to keep it real and keep people laughing.

But I’m more than just a comic. I run Triple B Outdoors, where I get to share my love for hunting, fishing, and the outdoors with folks who live for the same things I do. I’m engaged to the love of my life, and when I’m not on the road, you’ll find me in the woods, on the water, or working to grow my businesses.

At the end of the day, I’m not trying to be famous, I’m just trying to be honest, entertain people, and maybe inspire a few along the way. If you stick around, you’ll get jokes, a little wisdom, and probably a few stories you wouldn’t want your grandma to hear.

This is an 18+ event.

title: Declaration of Independence Curator Talks
description: The Declaration of Independence: The Motives, The Moment is an exhibition exploring the journey taken by the nation's founders. How colonial leaders go from loyal subjects of the British empire to risking everything by becoming revolutionaries? Take an insightful tour of the exhibition from the curator, with up close looks at Revolutionary War era correspondence, books and manuscripts that influenced the revolutionaries, and media that spread the word about the declaration.  Light refreshments will be offered. Come learn something new about the nation's founding and have a snack in Indiana University's rare book library!  Tours are offered at noon, 1 p.m., and 2 p.m. RSVP requested for planning of refreshments and staffing.

title: 15th Annual Indiana State Fingerstyle Guitar Festival
location: Brown County Playhouse, 70 S. Van Buren St, Nashville, IN, 47448, United States
description:
BUY TICKETS

The 15th Annual Indiana State Fingerstyle Guitar Festival features a morning guitar competition and an evening concert! Tickets are available separately for each, or an all-day pass is available.
Fingerstyle Guitar Competition:
11:00 AM – 2:00PM
Acoustic guitarists from around the world take the stage for the 15th annual Fingerstyle Guitar Competition. The competition features two rounds of performance, showcasing a wide range of styles, techniques, and original music. At the conclusion of the competition, the top three players will be awarded grand-prize guitars and earn the honor of performing in the Evening Concert.
Evening Concert:
7:30 PM
The Evening Concert features fingerstyle guitar legend Muriel Anderson, with special guests Tim Thompson and Jack Wilson. The concert will open with performances by the top three competition winners, giving audiences the chance to hear tomorrow’s guitar greats alongside established masters.
Muriel Anderson is internationally celebrated for her mastery of the 20-string harp-guitar, her versatility across musical genres, and her warm, engaging stage presence. She was the first woman to win the National Fingerstyle Guitar Championship, and Guitar Player Magazine has named her one of the Top 50 Guitarists of All Time. Recently recognized among the Top Ten Female Guitarists, Muriel’s lasting legacy lies in her uplifting compositions and deeply expressive performances.
She has recorded with country legend Chet Atkins, performed with Les Paul and countless other greats in “Muriel Anderson’s All Star Guitar Night®” over the past 30+ years. Muriel’s CD Nightlight Daylight placed first in its category in 11 national awards, followed by Acoustic Chef, an international CD with cookbook. Muriel’s release Sailing Dreams exhibits new levels of creativity–she has even created a board game to accompany the music.
Jack Wilson is a Chicagoland-based guitarist and 2013 Indiana Guitar Fest Champion. He won 1st place at the 2016 International Fingerstyle Guitar Competition at the Walnut Valley Festival after placing second in 2015. Appearing multiple times as a featured performer on our stage, Jack is a chameleon of a player, being able to shift comfortably from acoustic to electric, pick or fingerstyle in a wide range of styles, and immediately gets your attention by producing a cowbell sound from the guitar from his version of “Low Rider”.
Tim Thompson is a Nashville-based fingerstyle guitarist and a two-time International Fingerstyle Guitar Champion. Long associated with our guitar festival, Tim’s career includes extensive session work and collaborations across genres, appearing with artists like Tommy Emmanuel, Crystal Gayle, and Hwy 101. He has recorded eleven CDs and two DVDs. He has been featured in the Taylor Guitar’s Wood and Steel, Just Jazz Guitar and Fingerstyle Guitar which said, “Tim Thompson is a Nashville based finger-stylist that burns!”
Ticket Information
Front & Center Tickets are limited to the first four rows in the center section of the theater and offer the closest possible view of the stage.
BUY TICKETS

title: Curator-led Tour
location: SIDNEY AND LOIS ESKENAZI MUSEUM OF ART - Room Number: Check in with Guest Services on the first floor
description: Step into the world of acclaimed photographer Rania Matar (b. 1964, Lebanon), whose striking portraits document young women persisting in uncertain times. Matar's lens highlights women's creativity, strength, dignity, and resilience—offering a powerful counter-narrative to the challenges of her country and region. Read more about this featured exhibition (https://artmuseum.indiana.edu/exhibitions/upcoming/_feature-exhibitions/2026-03-05-rania-matar-where-do-i-go.html).  Join us for a rare opportunity to explore the artist's work in depth, guided by the scholar who researched and curated the exhibition. Read Leila Reichert's bio. (http://Elliot%20Josephine%20Leila%20Reichert%20is%20an%20American%20curator,%20critic,%20and%20editor.%20She%20is%20the%20inaugural%20Curator%20of%20Contemporary%20Art%20at%20the%20Eskenazi%20Museum%20of%20Art%20at%20Indiana%20University%20and%20was%20a%202023-24%20Propel%20Fellow%20with%20the%20Association%20of%20Art%20Museum%20Curators%20Foundation.%20She%20was%20formerly%20Art%20Editor%20of%20Newcity%20and%20Assistant%20Curator%20at%20the%20Block%20Museum%20of%20Art,%20Northwestern%20University.%20She%20has%20curated%20and%20assisted%20in%20developing%20critically%20celebrated%20exhibitions%20in%20the%20United%20States%20and%20abroad.%20Her%20critical%20writing%20has%20been%20published%20in%20the%20Journal%20of%20Visual%20Studies,%20Hyperallergic,%20the%20Brooklyn%20Rail,%20Newcity%20Art,%20and%20The%20Seen,%20among%20others.%20She%20holds%20dual%20masters%E2%80%99%20degrees%20in%20Modern%20and%20Contemporary%20Art%20History%20and%20Arts%20Administration%20and%20Cultural%20Policy%20from%20the%20School%20of%20the%20Art%20Institute%20of%20Chicago%20and%20a%20Bachelors%20of%20Arts%20with%20Honors%20in%20Art%20History%20from%20Northwestern%20University,%20where%20she%20graduated%20summa%20cum%20laude.)  Reserve your spot today for a chance to connect with Rania Matar's striking photographs in a personal, meaningful way. Leila will share the stories, research, and insights behind the exhibition, making this experience as memorable as the photography itself.Know Before You GoSpace is limited to 15 participants. RSVP to reserve a space. Walk-ins are welcome if space is available. If this page says "registration has closed", this means that registration and the waitlist have filled for this date and time.Plan to arrive early to check in. At 1:25 p.m., any unclaimed spaces may be released to guests who are standing by waiting for a space.Admission and tours are free.A small handbag may be carried at your side. Larger bags and backpacks will need to be stored before touring.Read about where to find us, where to park, accessibility, and museum guidelines.When You ArriveCheck in with Guest Services staff on the first floor.If you park in the IMU parking lot, Guest Services can validate your parking voucher for a discount.Be sure to store backpacks and large bags before touring. Lockers and coat racks are available.Keep space (18") between yourself, including anything you're carrying, and the art.
source: Eskenazi Museum of Art, IU Arts & Humanities Institute, IU Hamilton Lugar School

title: AI Applications in Marketing
description: 2026 Dates: August 4-August 27Tuesdays and Thursdays from 6 to 7:30 p.m. ETDelivery: Online, instructor-ledPrice: $1,995  Equip yourself with a GenAI toolset designed for impactful AI adoption. Anchored in the marketing process, this course empowers you with skills to leverage AI effectively, driving growth, reducing cycle times, and cutting costs. Dive deep into the market landscape, employ advanced analytics, create compelling content, and utilize GenAI tools for automation and monitoring. You'll culminate your learning by creating a personalized marketing AI assistant, all while mastering change management for competitive advantages.  Who is this for? Marketing practitioners and leaders who are hungry to get up to speed on AI for themselves, their teams, and organizations, and don't want to be left behind. Anchored in the marketing planning process, students will learn how AI can help answer marketing questions through hands-on learning and case studies. AI enables students to build apps and actions that were previously the domain of coders. No prior AI experience or technical experience is needed to take full advantage of the course.

title: Chamberfest Brown County: Letters From The Front
location: Brown County Playhouse, 70 S. Van Buren St, Nashville, IN, 47448, United States
description:
BUY TICKETS

Introducing New Music Celebrating History And Honoring Service
Letters From The Front is a new musical work for four voices and piano. Composed by New York composer Patrick Zimmerli with libretto by Mirabelle Ordinaire, this song cycle is based on letters sent by service members and their loved ones during wartime. Each piece is based on an actual letter written during one of our nation’s most crucial wars, including the American Revolution, the Civil War, World War I, World War II, Vietnam, and the Gulf War.
This new work will be released in the summer of 2026 to celebrate the 250th birthday of the United States.

Featuring:

ANGELA BORN – MEZZO-SOPRANO

DAVID GOVERTSEN – BASS-BARITONE

DESIRÉE HASSLER – SOPRANO

TYLER LEE – TENOR

LAN GINDES – PIANO

title: Tommy Emmanuel CGP - Living In The Light Tour
location: Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
Hailed as “one of the best acoustic guitarists in the world” by NPR’s World Cafe, Tommy Emmanuel got his start at the age of six, when he first began touring his native Australia with his family’s band. As a teenager, he earned a reputation as a highly sought after sideman and session player, and by his early twenties, Emmanuel was playing on chart-topping hits and performing with acts like Air Supply and Men at Work. Inspired in part by his hero, Chet Atkins (who would later become a friend, mentor, and collaborator), Emmanuel stepped out on his own as a solo artist in 1979, releasing the first in a string of acclaimed instrumental albums that would make him an unlikely celebrity in his home country and beyond. In the decades that followed, he would go on to headline everywhere from the Sydney Opera House to Carnegie Hall; tour with luminaries like Eric Clapton and John Denver; win a Grammy Award for Best Instrumental Arrangement; perform for a televised audience of more than two billion at the Sydney Olympics; and collaborate with a who’s who of fellow guitar greats, including Les Paul, Mark Knopfler, Joe Walsh, Richard Thompson, Jason Isbell, and Billy Strings.

Recorded in just four days, Emmanuel’s latest album, Living In The Light, stands as the most daring—and most rewarding—collection in the globetrotting fingerpicker’s remarkable catalog, fusing his pop, jazz, classical, and roots influences into a virtuosic masterwork as exhilarating as it is intimate.

Trey Hensley:

GRAMMY® winner and reigning two-time IBMA Guitar Player of the Year Trey Hensley has been called a “musical phenom” by NPR, “a guitar-slinging superhero” by Bluegrass Today, “Nashville’s hottest young player” by Acoustic Guitar magazine, and “the Swiss Army knife of roots music” by WMOT Radio’s Craig Havighurst. Hensley’s artistry has received high praise from the likes of Merle Haggard and Marty Stuart. An Eric Clapton’s Crossroads Guitar Festival invitee who has collaborated with Johnny Cash, Vince Gill, Dolly Parton, Taj Mahal and Tommy Emmanuel, Hensley is known for his other-worldly guitar playing, jaw-dropping musicianship and soulful storytelling. Having recently announced his signature guitar with Taylor Guitars, The Trey Hensley Gold Label 510e. Hensley is set to release a new album, Can’t Outrun The Blues, on March 6, 2026. The title cut has spent multiple weeks at #1 on the bluegrass charts.

title: Elvis: In Person Starring Matt Stone
location: Buskirk-Chumley Theater,114 East Kirkwood Avenue, Bloomington, IN 47408, USA
description:
The GREATEST ELVIS CONCERT EXPERIENCE IN AMERICA is RETURNING to the Buskirk-Chumley Theater on August 21st!

Internationally recognized by Graceland and the Elvis Presley Estate, the show’s star, Matt Stone, performs the CLOSEST THING to the King in history! Matt brings a never-before-seen level of authenticity and attention to detail to his craft, CAPTIVATING audiences with flawless recreation of the King of Rock ’n’ Roll in his prime!

He’s not an “Elvis impersonator,” but a talented vocalist portraying the GREATEST ENTERTAINER OF ALL TIME in a theatrical production— and after seeing this ELECTRIFYING SPECTACLE, you’ll remember ELVIS THE ICON, and forget the punchline.

He has earned the approval of Elvis’ friends and family, who have said:

“When I heard [Matt]… now if I close my eyes that’s Elvis! It touched my heart. The only person I ever heard sing like that was my brother, and [Matt] was real close—SCARY— that’s how close it was.” -Billy Stanley (Elvis’ Step Brother)

“Matt is incredibly gifted & handsome, with incredible energy and sex appeal.” – Joyce Bova (Longtime Elvis Girlfriend)

He has even been heard on SiriusXM’s ELVIS RADIO!

Backed by a sensational all-star band, Matt Stone MESMERIZES fans into blue-suede bliss as they’re HYPNOTIZED into thinking Elvis is BACK IN THE BUILDING at the PEAK of his prowess!

title: 2nd Weekend Work Day Trail Building
description:
Knobstone Hiking Trail Association of Indiana Meetup
Would you to spend a day in some beautiful woods with a delightful group of people building a connecting section of Indiana’s premier long distance hiking trail? Come join us as we continue work on the Tecumseh Trail extension portion of the KHT in MMSF. We will be building the new trail including clipping green briar.
Please bring a lunch and water. We will have large hand tools, but please bring any small tools you have such as clippers or a pruning saw. And a backpack is handy to carry everything in. Instructions will be provided for those who need them. We finish about 3:30.
Meet at the Hatfield Ridge Road gate. The gate is locked but we have a key. Go to the big sign on Rosenbaum/Catholic Cemetry Rd.
Join us on our website at http://www.knobstonehikingtrail.org/, on Facebook at [www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484](http://www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484), and Substack [https://khta.substack.com/](https://khta.substack.com/)
source: Meetup Knobstone Hiking Trail Association Of Indiana Meetup

title: WKU Fly Fishing Montana September 2026
description:
AND
September 20 –  September 25
Join WKU and experience fly fishing on world-renowned
waters which contain several trout species and
other fish such as mountain whitefish, northern
pikeminnow, and pike. Guides typically take
participants on one or more of the following
rivers: Blackfoot, Clark Fork, Bitterroot.
No prior fly-fishing experience is required.
This program is designed to meet the
needs of the complete beginner and ‘experts’
alike; whatever an expert is. All fishing will be
from drift boats or rafts. Being able to cast a
flyrod is certainly helpful, but not a requirement
as on-the-river instruction is readily available.
WKU has successfully offered this program since
2012 to participants with a wide range of abilities
(from no prior experience to being a fly-fishing
guide).
https://www.wku.edu/flyfishing/

title: Bloomington Community Farmers' Market
location: City of Bloomington, 401 N Morton St, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
The Bloomington Community Farmers' Market takes place rain or shine every Saturday, April through October, at Showers Common next to City Hall, 401 N. Morton St. in Bloomington.
April-September: 8 a.m.-12:30 p.m.
October: 9 a.m.-12:30 p.m.
November: 9 a.m.-12:30 p.m. at the Pavilion at Switchyard Park
The Market is a gathering place where local farmers, makers, and neighbors come together to share the foods, traditions, and connections that strengthen our community and support a reliable local food supply.
Market goers find a wide assortment of goods from the farm and field, including fruits and vegetables, eggs, meats and cheeses, honey, seasonal flowers, and annual and perennial plants. Food and beverage artisans provide a tasteful variety of drinks, pastries, and savory dishes. This weekly gathering provides opportunities for individuals to connect with their community in meaningful ways and support local farmers and small businesses.

title: 31st Annual Run for the Animals 5K
description:
Please join us on Sunday, October 4th, for MCHA's 31st Annual Run for the Animals presented by The Dog House!

Learn more & register at monroehumane.org/5k

Run for the Animals is a timed 5K run/walk at Karst Farm Park, with a portion of the course winding through the Monroe County Fairgrounds. All of the proceeds benefit the programs and services of Monroe County Humane Association.

NEW in 2026, we're introducing separate start times for runners and walkers! The course starts at Karst Farm Park and loops through the Monroe County Fairgrounds. The awards ceremony and sponsor booths are now located near the start/finish line, making it easier for participants and families to celebrate together—and for faster results posting! After the awards, stick around for the raffles and fan-favorite pet contests. You don't have to be registered in the 5K to participate in the raffle and pet contests!

This event is so important to MCHA and our ability to provide programs and services that would otherwise not exist in our community, including accessible veterinary care, spay/neuter assistance, crisis housing, emergency pet food & supply pantry, street outreach, and more. MCHA is a private 501c3 non-profit that receives no funding from national organizations or tax dollars.

We anticipate welcoming hundreds of runners, walkers, and canine friends to this event! Don’t miss this great opportunity to support animal welfare in our community!

EVENT SCHEDULE:

Saturday, October 3
10:00 AM–12:00 PM & 5:00–7:00 PM: Preregistered Packet Pick-up at Karst Farm Park Commons Bldg

Sunday, October 4
8:00 AM: Packet Pick-up & Onsite Registration Opens
9:15 AM: Registration Closes, Trainer-led Group Stretch
9:30 AM: 5K Run Begins
9:35 AM: 5K Walk Begins
10:30 AM: 5K Run/Walk Awards Ceremony
11:00 AM: Pet Contests & Raffle Winners Announced

5K Award Categories:

Top 3 Overall Men's 5K
Top 3 Overall Women's 5K
Top 3 Youth (12 & under) Male 5K
Top 3 Youth (12 & under) Female 5K
Top Male & Top Female 13-17 years
Top Male & Top Female 18-24 years
Top Male & Top Female 25-29 years
Top Male & Top Female 30-39 years
Top Male & Top Female 40-49 years
Top Male & Top Female 50-59 years
Top Male & Top Female 60+ years
Top 3 Canine 5K

Important Notice for Canine Participants: As required by the state of Indiana, all dogs must be up to date on the rabies vaccination. In addition, only spayed/neutered dogs are permitted to participate. (Visit monroehumane.org for information on Spay/Neuter Assistance.) All dogs must remain on a leash at all times during the event. For safety reasons, retractable leashes are not permitted—only standard, non-retractable leashes are allowed. Thank you for helping us ensure a safe and fun event for everyone!

👏👏👏 THANK YOU TO OUR SPONSORS:

PRESENTING SPONSOR: The 31st Annual Run for the Animals is presented by The Dog House, offering cage-free boarding in a cozy, home-like environment. With 24-hour staffing and personalized care, The Dog House strives to be your pet’s home away from home. Learn more at trydoghouse.com.

GOLD SPONSORS: Monroe County Parks and Recreation & Monroe County Fairgrounds
SILVER SPONSORS: BloomingPaws; German American Bank; & Slotegraaf Niehoff P.C.
BRONZE SPONSORS: IU Credit Union & Smithville

Market goers find a wide assortment of goods from the farm and field, including fruits and vegetables, eggs, meats and cheeses, honey, seasonal flowers, and annual and perennial plants. Food and beverage artisans provide a tasteful variety of drinks, pastries, and savory dishes. This weekly gathering provides opportunities for individuals to connect with their community in meaningful ways and support local farmers and small businesses.

title: Indianapolis World Oddities Expo
description:
Welcome to WOE’s 2nd Indianapolis event —prepare yourself for an experience like no other!

🗓️ Saturday & Sunday, November 7th & 8th
📍 Indiana State Fairgrounds & Event Center
🦇 Ag/Hort Building
🎟️ VIP 10am-7pm
🎟️ General Admission 11am-7pm
Children 12 and under are free!

👁🎪Welcome to the World Oddities Expo- a traveling celebration of all things weird, intriguing, and magically macabre! We’re combining our love of art, history, science and entertainment wrapped into one incredible event! Shop till you drop from dozens of eclectic vendors and small businesses. Grab a ticket to the Deformed Exhibit to indulge in rare, preserved specimens that defy the laws of nature! Take a workshop in WOE labs and try your hand at taxidermy, rat skeleton articulation, or create a miniature diorama terrarium and more! Visit our Main Stage for amazing live performances throughout the day, as well as surprise guest speakers for free seminars! Whether you’re into oddities, art, fashion, science, or the culture- this is your space to celebrate what makes you unique!

💀👻DEFORMED EXHIBIT
Visit our touring, one-of-a-kind Deformed Exhibit featuring real, rare specimens with medical anomalies! Snag a combo general admission ticket or a combo VIP ticket for those who want early access to shop before the crowds.

🦋🔬WOE LABS
Ready to kickstart your new favorite hobby but aren’t quite sure how to start? Take a class at WOE Labs and enter a world where learning and creativity collide! Our knowledgeable and experienced instructors are there to guide you step by step. Don’t stress- all experience levels are welcomed! Classes vary from city to city.

✨️🎭 LIVE ENTERTAINMENT
We curate only the best and most talented performers to take the WOE stage! You’ll find everything from contortion, odes to sideshow, clowns, as well as a few things you’ve never seen before! Don't miss these jaw dropping performances at the WOE Main Stage throughout the day!

✍️🩸 ODDITY INK PARLOUR- Only in select cities* Grab some new ink at the Oddity Ink Parlour and get a tattoo on site! Sponsored by Villain Arts and only in select cities- we’re bringing you talented artists from all over the country. Grab those VIP tickets so you can be first in line for an appointment!
This isn’t just an expo; it’s an experience, and you’re part of it. Get ready to join a community of people who celebrate the strange, the wonderful, and the weird. We can’t wait to see you there—stay weird, friend!

🎟️ ➡️ Get your tickets NOW at WORLDODDITIESEXPO.COM

https://www.facebook.com/events/2006418706819661/

title: Board of Park Commissioners Meeting
location: City of Bloomington, 401 N Morton St, Bloomington, IN 47404, USA
description:
The Board of Park Commissioners is a policy-making authority for the City of Bloomington Parks and Recreation Department. The Board is comprised of four members appointed by the Mayor.
Statutory Authority: (I.C. 36-10-4) (BMC 2.20)
The Board meets at Bloomington's City Hall at 5 p.m. on the third Thursday of the month, with some exceptions for holidays.
See meeting agendas and notes from the Board of Park Commissioners online at https://bloomington.in.gov/boards/park-commissioners
For more information about the Board of Park Commissioners, send an email to Parks and Recreation Department Director Tim Street or call 812-349-3700.

title: 2nd Weekend Work Day Trail Building
description:
Knobstone Hiking Trail Association of Indiana Meetup
Would you to spend a day in some beautiful woods with a delightful group of people building a connecting section of Indiana’s premier long distance hiking trail? Come join us as we continue work on the Tecumseh Trail extension portion of the KHT in MMSF. We will be building the new trail including clipping green briar.
Please bring a lunch and water. We will have large hand tools, but please bring any small tools you have such as clippers or a pruning saw. And a backpack is handy to carry everything in. Instructions will be provided for those who need them. We finish about 3:30.
Meet at the Hatfield Ridge Road gate. The gate is locked but we have a key. Go to the big sign on Rosenbaum/Catholic Cemetry Rd.
Join us on our website at http://www.knobstonehikingtrail.org/, on Facebook at [www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484](http://www.facebook.com/groups/2255586117953484), and Substack [https://khta.substack.com/](https://khta.substack.com/)
source: Meetup Knobstone Hiking Trail Association Of Indiana Meetup

EVERY. FIRST. FRIDAY. Experience an exciting night of the arts as fourteen distinct galleries stay open late to host special exhibits and serve refreshments from 5-8pm.

Stroll the vibrant arts scene along the sensational art mile and experience amazing art by renowned local, regional, and international artists. Discover delightful surprises, inspiration, festivities, and original works of art for purchase. Explore each distinctive gallery in-person or online all year long at your own pace.

Click here to learn more https://www.gallerywalkbloomington.com/
